zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand於1490年出生在英國米德萊可斯特普尼時，她的父親William Campyn20歲，母親F Campyn18歲。她於1518年11月17日與Henry Stephens在英國英格蘭倫敦結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1580年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾享年90歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand于1490年出生在英国米德萊可斯特普尼时，她的父亲William Campyn20岁，母亲F Campyn18岁。她于1518年11月17日与Henry Stephens在英国英格兰倫敦结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1580年在英国英格兰奧可福爾享年90岁。

nameMeanings.name: Campyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch and North German: variant of   Kampen   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

English (Essex, of Norman origin): variant of Campion or Campain. The latter is a habitational name from any of several places in Pas-de-Calais and elsewhere in France named Campagne, or from a Norman form of a habitational name from Champagne in northeastern France.

variants.name: Kampen
meanings: Dutch (mainly Van Kampen): habitational name for someone from the city of Kampen in the province of Overijssel, or from any other of the places in the Netherlands with that name.
North German: topographic name from the plural or an inflected form of Low German kamp ‘enclosed field’ (see Kamp 1), or a habitational name from Kampen on the island of Sylt in Schleswig-Holstein.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads in southeastern Norway named Kampen, from the definite singular form of kamp ‘round hill top, boulder’.

nameMeanings.name: Marchand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Pierre, Emile, Jacques, Lucien, Normand, Alphonse, Camille, Cecile, Colette.

French: occupational name for a buyer and seller of goods, from Old French march(e)ant, Late Latin mercatans, from Latin mercari ‘to trade’, from merx ‘commerce, exchange, merchandise’. In the Middle Ages the term was used mainly to denote a wholesale dealer. Compare Marchant and Mershon .
English (London): variant of   Marchant  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Merchan
meanings: Spanish (Merchán): occupational name from merchán ‘merchant, dealer’, from Old French merchant (see Marchand ).

variants.name: Merchant
meanings: English: occupational name for a buyer and seller of goods, from Old French, Middle English march(e)ant, Late Latin mercatans (see Marchand ).
Indian (Gujarat and Mumbai): Muslim and Parsi occupational name for a trader, from the English vocabulary word merchant.

variants.name: Marchant
meanings: English (southern): occupational name from Middle English marchaunt ‘merchant, trader, shopkeeper’ (Old French marchand, marchëant).
Altered form of French   Marchand  , a cognate of 1 above.

History: John Marchant (c. 1600–c. 1668) was in Newport, RI, before 1638. In that year he moved to Braintree, MA, then to Watertown, MA (1642), and finally to Yarmouth, MA (1648). His descendants included many sea captains and other prominent people.

variants.name: Marquand
meanings: French and Channel Islands (Guernsey): variant of French   Marchand   and a cognate of English   Marchant  .

nameMeanings.name: Frances
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Francis .  In the 16th century the two spellings were used indiscriminately for both sexes, the distinction in spelling not being established until the 17th century.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand was born in 1490, in Stepney, Middlesex, England, her father, William Campyn, was 20 and her mother, F Campyn, was 18. She married Henry Stephens on 17 November 1518, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1580, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 90.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand est née en 1490, à Stepney, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, William Campyn, avait 20 ans et sa mère, F Campyn, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Stephens le 17 novembre 1518, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1580, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 90 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand(이)가 1490년 영국 미들레세스 스테프니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Campyn(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 F Campyn(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1518년 11월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 Henry Stephens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1580년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.
